item.title: Startup Hadrian Has Discussed New Funding at $7.5 Billion Value
item.description: Company runs AI-powered factories that aim to speed up manufacturing

item.title: Macklem Says Bank Capital Rule Change Alone Won’t Spark Lending
item.description: Looser bank capital rules alone won’t boost economic activity, according to Bank of Canada Governor Tiff Macklem, echoing analysts who questioned whether a recent regulatory change would immediately spur more lending without borrowers at the door.

item.title: Argentine Economy Grew More Than Expected in First Quarter
item.description: Economic growth in Argentina proved resilient in the first quarter, surpassing expectations even as unemployment rose and wages trailed inflation.

item.title: Cboe Revives Binary Options to Compete with Kalshi, Polymarket
item.description: Cboe Global Markets, Inc. announced Tuesday that it has brought back a type of S&P 500 contract after a hiatus of more than a decade, which in the meantime has become a popular mainstay of prediction market platforms Kalshi and Polymarket.

item.title: JPMorgan’s New Credit Funds Win SEC Nod for Monthly Redemptions
item.description: JPMorgan Chase & Co. won approval to offer monthly redemptions on a new interval fund investing in private and public credit, as the direct-lending industry grapples with another round of elevated requests to pull out cash.

item.title: Ether-Linked Nonprofit Cuts Budget 40% During ‘Challenging Era’
item.description: Vitalik Buterin, the programmer who helped create underlying technology for Ether, the world’s No. 2 cryptocurrency, said the nonprofit behind it is being forced to make some difficult decisions.

item.title: Meta Building Prediction Market App That Wouldn’t Wager Money
item.description: Meta Platforms Inc. Chief Executive Officer Mark Zuckerberg has directed a small team to create a prediction market application similar to Kalshi Inc. and Polymarket, but with lower stakes as users won’t likely wager real money.

item.title: A Jevons Paradox in Bureaucracy
item.description: Will AI boost productivity?

item.title: Quantum Computing Is Finally Here. But What Is It?
item.description: Four decades ago, physicists were theorizing that the mind-bending mechanics of quantum physics could be harnessed to make a new kind of computer that’s exponentially more powerful than conventional machines. A series of breakthroughs has now brought “quantum utility” closer to reality. A race is on to develop machines that are accurate enough to faithfully model the behavior of complex real-world phenomena and deliver a leap forward in fields as varied as drug development, financial modeling an

item.title: Korea AI Rout Revives Scrutiny of $290 Billion Levered-ETF Boom
item.description: South Korea’s AI-fueled selloff has put a spotlight back on one of the fastest-growing corners of retail investing: leveraged exchange-traded funds.

item.title: Schick Razor Maker Edgewell’s Shares Leap on Rejected Offer
item.description: Shares of Edgewell Personal Care Co., which makes Schick razors, rose nearly 18% in New York on Tuesday after the company rejected an unsolicited takeover offer from private equity firm Yellow Wood Partners.

item.title: Dollar Jumps to Highest Since November on Fed Rate Hike Bets
item.description: The dollar touched the highest level since November on Tuesday as traders cement views on the Federal Reserve hiking interest rates this year.

item.title: Alibaba Sues the US, Seeking Removal From Pentagon’s Blacklist
item.description: Alibaba Group Holding Ltd. sued the Department of Defense to be removed from a blacklist that identifies the e-commerce leader as supporter of the Chinese military, appealing to the US justice system to avoid a designation the company says is arbitrary and unjustified.

item.title: Anthropic Wants Claude to Be Your New Slack Coworker
item.description: Anthropic PBC wants Claude to enter the Slack chat in a bigger way.

item.title: Trump Call for Crop Sales to Iran Adds Cautious Hope for Farmers
item.description: President Donald Trump on Tuesday reiterated that funds from any unfrozen Iranian assets would be used in part for grain shipments, potentially providing a fresh market for American farmers.

item.title: Pemex and Petrobras to Team Up on Oil and Refining Projects
item.description: The national oil companies of Mexico and Brazil agreed to work together to discover, produce and refine oil as both Latin American energy giants push to expand their reserves.

item.title: EG Group Confidentially Files for $1 Billion US IPO
item.description: EG Group, the gas station and convenience store operator, has confidentially filed for a US initial public offering that could raise about $1 billion, people familiar with the matter said.

item.title: Treasuries Gain Before Auctions as Traders Trim Rate Hike Bets
item.description: Treasuries rose as a selloff in US stocks and further declines in oil prices curbed wagers on Federal Reserve interest-rate increases over the coming year.

item.title: SpaceX Finds Both Demand, Questions for Its Debut Bond Offering
item.description: SpaceX met with plenty of fanfare when it first sold shares this month, making history with the biggest ever initial public offering. In the debt markets, it’s encountering more questions.

item.title: Netflix Unveils New Horror Game to Jumpstart Interactive Offerings
item.description: The company wants ‘Unhinged’ to be a gateway to other video games on the streaming service

item.title: Russia Considers Diesel-Export Ban on Ukraine Refinery Attacks
item.description: Russia is weighing a ban on exports of diesel to avoid domestic shortages after Ukraine intensified attacks on the nation’s refineries.

item.title: Cyber Firm Varonis Weighs Sale After Takeover Interest
item.description: Cybersecurity company Varonis Systems Inc. is exploring options including a potential sale after receiving takeover interest, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Maritime Watchdog to Start Evacuating Stranded Seafarers From Middle East
item.description: The International Maritime Organization said it has received safety guarantees that could allow hundreds of ships to exit the Persian Gulf through the Strait of Hormuz, as it seeks to evacuate thousands of seafarers.

item.title: Lula Weighs Replacing Senate Leader Amid Banco Master Fallout
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva sees Senator Jaques Wagner as a growing liability to his reelection campaign after a police investigation drew his longtime ally into Brazil’s biggest banking fraud probe, according to a person with knowledge of the matter.

item.title: Israel Weighs US IPO for Multi-Billion-Dollar Defense Companies
item.description: Israel is considering listing state-owned defense giants Israel Aerospace Industries Ltd. and Rafael Advanced Defense Systems Ltd. in the US to potentially avoid stricter disclosure obligations in the local market.

item.title: ECB’s Vujcic Sees Inflation Remaining Higher for Longer
item.description: The European Central Bank’s decision to raise interest rates was “robust” considering inflation will stay higher for longer, according to Vice President Boris Vujcic.

item.title: US Sanctions Key Cuban Bank and Miner in Fresh Blow to Havana
item.description: The US sanctioned the Cuban military’s international banking arm and a state-run company that’s partnered with Trafigura Group and other miners, in the latest escalation of pressure against Havana’s communist leadership.

item.title: Massachusetts Court Kills Proposal for Toughest US Rent Control
item.description: The Massachusetts Supreme Judicial Court struck down a ballot proposal that would have imposed the strictest statewide rent control in the US.

item.title: Sequoia, Founders Fund Back Kamikaze Drone Startup Stark at €3.5 Billion Valuation
item.description: Stark Defence, a startup making kamikaze drones, has raised €500 million ($570 million) in a fundraising round led by Sequoia Capital and Peter Thiel’s Founders Fund as private capital pours into European military technology.

item.title: US Natural Gas Falls on Cooler Outlooks, Smaller LNG Flows
item.description: US natural gas futures declined as forecasts shifted to show cooler weather in the coming weeks, suggesting a slight decline in demand for gas-fired electricity as consumers pull back on using air-conditioners. US gas flows to liquefied natural gas export facilities also fell, increasing supply in the domestic market.
